summary: nodemon-gulp republishes upstream nodemon source under an unaffiliated name
details: nodemon-gulp@3.1.14 ships a verbatim copy of the legitimate `nodemon` package: identical lib/ tree, bin/nodemon.js, windows-kill.exe, and README pointing at nodemon.io and the remy/nodemon repository. The package.json declares name `nodemon-gulp` with `main:./lib/nodemon` and `bin.nodemon:./bin/nodemon.js`, but the publisher is not affiliated with the upstream nodemon maintainer. No install hooks, no network exfiltration, no obfuscation, and no other installer-harm payload is present in this version — the current code is functionally upstream nodemon. The concern is name confusion (a developer reaching for nodemon or a gulp-flavored variant may install this instead) combined with the publisher's ability to ship a malicious future version under a name that already has install footprint. Routing for human review to assess name-confusion intent and registry takedown options.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
